mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent and monthly housing starts rising above 1.2 million units indicate broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.5% in forward returns, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 1.1 percent reflects rising manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly improves corporate operating leverage and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading that exceeds 2.5 percent annualized triggers Fed tightening expectations, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX declines in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales and food services revenue indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS changes and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.1, or forward returns are negative in the top quartile of RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly employment gains exceeding 200,000 and industrial production growth above 0.8 percent signal synchronized labor and supply expansion, which reduces input cost volatility and boosts equity risk premiums, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a predictive regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10), or the joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units act as a leading indicator for construction-related economic activity and consumer wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon when the signal activates.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 65% of instances where HOUST crosses 1500, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline GDP growth model.
